current_fact.fact_text: On 9 May, many migratory birds pass over the Northern Hemisphere at night, navigating by starlight and the Earth’s magnetic field. Tiny crystals of magnetite in their tissues and quantum tricks in their eyes help them sense direction, turning the dark sky into a map their bodies can read.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

current_fact.match_reason: Night‑migrating birds literally live a kind of moving meditation across continents, perfectly in tune with an episode about travel, rhythm, and quiet inner shifts while in motion.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1926, Richard E. Byrd and Floyd Bennett claimed to have become the first people to fly over the North Pole in a Fokker tri-motor airplane. Decades later, Byrd’s recovered diary—with altered timings and course notes—sparked ongoing debate about whether they truly reached the Pole or turned back short of it.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

published.match_reason: An Arctic exploration flight pairs naturally with an episode built around polar waters, cold light, and cinematic awe beyond the polar circle.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1941, the German submarine U-110 was captured by the Royal Navy in the North Atlantic. Hidden inside was a working Enigma machine and codebooks, a jackpot that allowed Allied cryptographers to read secret German naval messages and quietly tilt the information war in their favor.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: Codebreaking and intelligence feel like a shadowy, cinematic puzzle; trip‑hop’s murky textures and nocturnal mood mirror the secret war around Enigma.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1502, Christopher Columbus set sail from Cádiz on his fourth and final voyage, still hunting for a westward sea route to Asia. Instead, storms, shipworms, and resistance from local peoples turned it into his harshest journey, bringing him to the coasts of Central America and leaving him marooned in Jamaica for a year.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

published.match_reason: A perilous sea voyage connects well with an episode featuring "Ma come fanno i marinai" (how sailors manage), blending travel, landscape, and seafaring imagery.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1540, Spanish navigator Hernando de Alarcón set sail toward the Gulf of California on a mission to resupply Coronado's inland expedition. He followed the lower Colorado River farther than any European before him, realizing that the long‑imagined Strait of Anián—a shortcut to Asia—did not exist there.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: An expedition up unknown waterways fits an aquatic, flowing set built around immersion, tides of sound, and journeys below the surface.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1960, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration announced approval of the oral contraceptive Enovid as a birth control pill. It became the world’s first approved oral contraceptive, quietly reshaping family planning, gender roles, and the politics of reproductive freedom.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

published.match_reason: A milestone in women’s bodily autonomy resonates with an episode celebrating powerful female artists and unapologetic feminine voices.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1980, the bulk carrier MV Summit Venture struck a support pier of Florida’s Sunshine Skyway Bridge during a sudden storm, bringing down a 430‑meter section of roadway. Cars and a bus plunged into Tampa Bay, killing 35 people and sparking major changes in bridge design and marine navigation safety.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

published.match_reason: A tragedy triggered by a sudden storm and low visibility finds an echo in an episode about rain, weathering hard transitions, and emerging with cautious hope.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 2001, a football match at Accra Sports Stadium in Ghana turned deadly when police fired tear gas into the stands after crowd unrest. The resulting panic and crush killed 129 people, making it Africa’s worst football stadium disaster and prompting serious scrutiny of crowd control and policing at sporting events.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

published.match_reason: A major tragedy in Ghanaian football history is paired with an episode that celebrates West African music and Ghana’s own highlife roots, honoring the region’s cultural resilience.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1920, Polish forces commanded by General Edward Rydz-Śmigły marked their capture of Kiev with a victory parade along Khreshchatyk. The triumph was short‑lived: the city would soon be retaken by Soviet troops, illustrating how fleeting control can be in a fluid, post‑imperial landscape.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

published.match_reason: A pivotal Polish military episode aligns with a personal musical journey across modern Poland, connecting history, landscape, and contemporary culture.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1761, the Society of Artists of Great Britain opened its first public exhibition at Spring Gardens in London. For the first time, British artists independently organized to show their work, helping to create an art‑market culture and paving the way for institutions like the Royal Academy.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

published.match_reason: The inaugural exhibition that launched an artistic movement mirrors Sedna FM’s own first episode—the starting point of a creative, collaborative platform.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1945, the Channel Islands became the last part of the British Isles to be liberated from Nazi occupation. After nearly five years of isolation, scarcity, and quiet resistance, islanders watched British ships return and raised homemade flags they had hidden throughout the war.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

published.match_reason: The gentle, slow relief of liberation after a long occupation fits an episode about exhaling, slowing down, and re‑entering peace after tension.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1386, England and Portugal sealed the Treaty of Windsor, formalizing an alliance that still exists today. It is often cited as the oldest continuous diplomatic alliance in the world, surviving dynastic changes, empire building, and world wars.

episode.title: Morning Drops #06 - Friend's Home
episode.description: Yasmin and Gaia are reunited under the same roof to spend Easter Monday morning together, exploring that heartwarming 'just like home' feeling that comes from being surrounded by beloved friends.

published.match_reason: An alliance that endures for centuries is like a friendship that becomes a second home, matching an episode about shared roofs, warmth, and long‑lasting bonds.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1974, the U.S. House Judiciary Committee opened televised impeachment hearings into President Richard Nixon’s conduct during the Watergate scandal. For the first time, millions of viewers could watch in real time as lawmakers dissected presidential abuses of power.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

published.match_reason: Publicly re‑examining presidential power mirrors the episode’s theme of questioning assumptions and learning to ‘think again’ about what we accept as true.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1955, West Germany was formally admitted to NATO, symbolizing its integration into a Western security framework just a decade after World War II. The move reshaped Cold War alliances and triggered the creation of the Warsaw Pact in response.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

published.match_reason: A pivotal moment in modern German history is paired with a lighthearted voyage with German friends, connecting national turning points to everyday German stories at sea.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1918, during World War I, the Royal Navy launched the Second Ostend Raid in an attempt to block the German‑held Belgian port of Ostend. Navigational errors and fierce resistance meant the blockships were sunk off target, showing how even meticulously planned coastal operations could go awry.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: A high‑risk naval raid full of adrenaline and chaos fits with a charged, guitar‑driven ‘electric shock’ set that feels like tactical action in sound.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1873, the Vienna stock exchange crashed in an event known as "Der Krach," sparking the Panic of 1873. The sudden collapse of speculative investments ushered in a years‑long global downturn remembered as the Long Depression, long before the Great Depression entered history books.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra

published.match_reason: A speculative financial bubble bursting is a cautionary tale about excess and automation; the episode’s call for intentional, less algorithm‑driven choices offers a quieter, more careful counter‑rhythm.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1662, the mischievous puppet character who became known as Mr. Punch made his first recorded appearance in England. Over time, Punch and Judy shows grew into a raucous seaside tradition, mixing slapstick violence and social satire in front of generations of children.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: Punch’s anarchic, theatrical playfulness matches an upbeat, mindset‑shifting episode that treats life a bit like a chaotic stage you can reimagine.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1310, Nephon, Bishop of Cyzicus, was consecrated as the Ecumenical Patriarch of Constantinople. His short tenure came during a turbulent era of Byzantine politics and church intrigue, with patriarchs often caught between emperors, monastic movements, and shifting theological debates.

episode.title: Morning Drops #28 - Under Scottish Skies
episode.description: Gaia shares a personal moment of transition — a shift in seasons, in rhythm, and in life. From this space of quiet change comes a musical journey through Scotland: not the usual names, but voices that speak from the edges. A personal ritual and a small act of curiosity: an invitation to notice what grounds us when everything moves.
songs: Paolo Nutini - Pencil Full of Lead

published.match_reason: A leadership transition in a spiritual center aligns with an episode about navigating change and seeking what grounds you when institutions and seasons shift.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 2022, U.S. President Joe Biden signed the Ukraine Democracy Defense Lend-Lease Act, reviving a World War II–era mechanism to speed military aid to Ukraine and other Eastern European allies. The law symbolically linked the 21st‑century Russo‑Ukrainian conflict to earlier struggles against authoritarian expansion.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

published.match_reason: A law consciously modeled on WWII policy invites reflection on learning from history; the episode’s focus on learning, persistence, and rethinking our responses fits that intergenerational feedback loop.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1987, LOT Polish Airlines Flight 5055 "Tadeusz Kościuszko" crashed near Warsaw after an engine failure led to an in‑flight fire and loss of control. All 183 people on board died, and the disaster spurred investigations that exposed serious design and maintenance issues in the aircraft’s engines.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

published.match_reason: A devastating aviation disaster calls for a contemplative frame about fragility, recovery, and looking back to understand what went wrong and how to heal.

published.fact_text: On 9 May 1980, in Norco, California, five heavily armed robbers held up a Security Pacific Bank, triggering one of the most violent police chases in state history. The so‑called Norco shootout involved homemade explosives, military‑style weapons, a helicopter, and left two robbers, a deputy, and dozens of vehicles destroyed.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: A chaotic, militarized confrontation with police echoes the explosive, anti‑authoritarian energy of a Rage Against the Machine–focused episode.

published.fact_text: Also on 9 May, the fictional planet of Sedna FM records that some Earth listeners discovered puppet shows, stock crashes, polar flights, and lost submarines in a single scroll of history. On this distant world, May 9 is celebrated as a reminder that one terrestrial date can contain voyages, disasters, revolutions, and quiet acts of art all at once.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

published.match_reason: A meta-fact about the abundance of stories in one date suits the open, boundary‑less spirit of the Evening Flows introduction, inviting listeners to drift across all these histories.

published.fact_text: On 9 May, while Europe marks the end of war and the birth of new alliances, forests in the far north quietly record another kind of history in their rings. Each year’s growth band stores data about temperature, rainfall, and even volcanic eruptions, allowing scientists to reconstruct centuries of climate change without a single written word.

published.fact_text: On 9 May in some years, Jupiter appears at opposition, rising as the Sun sets and shining at its brightest in Earth’s night sky. In a small telescope, its cloud bands and four largest moons become clearly visible, a reminder that even familiar planets turn into rich, layered worlds when you look a little closer.
